content:
2 min read
Just now

--

I know right now using A.I. is all the rage. Putting aside that I am a Michigan girl who loves her sand dunes, and how it’s really bad for our environment. Many founders will forgo this warning to save money, as starting a business is not a cheap endeavor. Cutting costs and strategically spending money are important parts of surviving your first year.
Don’t use A.I. where you seek to be unique.
It’s there in the name ‘Artificial Intelligence’, all it can do is copy what other humans have already done. God-made will beat out Man-made. You can tell when a graphic has been generated by A.I., no matter the platform, so if you seek to have a unique tone in your writing. If you seek a distinct design for your marketing. If you seek to differentiate yourself from the rest of the world. Go human.
Don’t use A.I. on data that needs to be protected.
A.I. will read, learn, and share the contents of documents you upload to its servers. I shudder to think how many interns (or unqualified management) upload classified, company-eyes-only information to A.I. to do their daily work, spilling company confidential information for the rest of the world to see if they simply dare to ask A.I.
Don’t use A.I. on tasks you don’t specialize in.
You’ll need to quickly review the results of A.I.’s efforts to determine if it’s good or not. It makes mistakes because the information that influences it is flawed. You need the expertise to go in and make changes to the results.
OGiDesigns is a Detroit-based full-service Creative Agency. I focus on helping entrepreneurs survive their first year, thrive by their third year, and revive their brand any year.
